connections.gloss: [phrase] avenge, [idiom] that condemn, contend, defend, execute (judgment), (be a) judge(-ment), [idiom] needs, plead, reason, rule.
avenge, conclude, condemn, damn, decree, determine, esteem, judge, go to (sue at the) law, ordain, call in question, sentence to, think
connections.def: properly, to distinguish, i.e. decide (mentally or judicially); by implication, to try, condemn, punish:--avenge, conclude, condemn, damn, decree, determine, esteem, judge, go to (sue at the) law,…
from 2919; a judge (genitive case or specially):--judge. see GREEK for

connections.gloss: contend, make (to) differ(-ence), discern, doubt, judge, be partial, stagger, waver
connections.def: from 1223 and 2919; to separate thoroughly, i.e. (literally and reflexively) to withdraw from, or (by implication) oppose; figuratively, to discriminate (by implication, decide), or (reflexively)…
from a derivative of 1349; a judger:--judge. see GREEK for
